title: Botox Injection Patterns: My GO-TO Patterns & The Ones You Must AVOID! [Aesthetics Mastery Show]
filename: Botox Injection Patterns： My GO-TO Patterns & The Ones You Must AVOID! [Aesthetics Mastery Show].en.vtt
excerpt: We're all searching for the universal formula to solve all problems and unfortunately life doesn't work that way welcome to the aesthetics mastery show the show that helps you raise the bar and thrive in your aesthetics career i'm dotson pierce hi I'm Randy pious and what are we …

segments.text: we're all searching for the universal formula to solve all problems and unfortunately life doesn't work that way welcome to the aesthetics mastery show the show that helps you raise the bar and thrive in your aesthetics career i'm dotson pierce hi I'm Randy pious and what are we talking about today Amanda okay it's a corona free zone and we're gonna be talking about Botox patents because it's something that comes up a lot on this give me between E network our closed Facebook group and it's I think people want there to be a wrote way to learn it but I don't think there is so I'm gonna have I want to dive into your brain and pull out the best way to do Botox yeah I mean absolutely right you are completely right and it's actually not just around Botox we have this problem this is how minds work in general which is we're all searching for the universal formula to solve all problems and unfortunately

life doesn't work that way what you need are universal principles to adjust each problem really uniquely so that this is what you realize as you go forward when I first started I was just like everyone else I want to know where to place the five injections so I can do the same on everyone and they'll all get the same lovely result just tell me yeah and unfortunately you soon discover that there might be an average face that you can actually get a get away with if you treat only average faces you there is actually a universal formula but unfortunately your formula will completely fall apart as soon as you meet someone who's just a little bitty the side of average which is more than 50% of people probably yeah okay let's dive in so what what's the crack with the license down so this is unlicensed Oaks yeah so the license is actually a really good way of understanding what's actually going on in terms of average faces because what the license is a pattern of injection that will suit the average face all right and give you a consistent result it may not necessarily even be

the best possible result for the average face but it's a consistent result they want to create consistent predictable results so that they can say to the FDA here's my product this is what it does we've tested it 3% of people it didn't do what we said 97% of people did exactly what it said so and they'll look at that and say that's a good ratio you've got a good nice result we'll give you that license and off you go and you teach everyone to do the same injection pattern because on 97% of people or whatever it is and you will get the result that your product is saying it will provide this FDA approval the licensing dose have they been conservative no well possibly they've been generous too because it means you use more of their product right so it's because actually that's something we should talk about with the forehead which is I think the forehead Botox dose is actually relatively high compared with what I use and I can get really good and I have been for 10 11 years now 12 years I've been getting really good results using less and what's the license dose says that you should use so that's

worth knowing because really what we want is to be able to operate in our clinic well and safely and you don't necessarily have to use what the product says I'm probably the licenses up to 20 units overthought anyway and but it but the way that you're taught is five injections of four units which is 20 in each area so when do we differ from the license dose so it's important to know that the license dose is it's quite specific for what it's for so it will say to soften lines and wrinkles what it doesn't say is to also lift the eyebrows lightly or and you know to avoid making the cheek look a bit saggy you know it's all sorts of things that are nuanced on either side of the license dose that are worth knowing about and so I would say the average client that I treat actually doesn't get exactly the license but we're pretty close to it so I mean they're in the same ballpark but there are little adjustments I make to make sure that the individual gets the best result possible because the license is really for the average of individuals and each person that you see is actually

a unique individual so it's like so many things like even seatbelts a good example and they're actually built it's kind of one of these things that's used to illustrate and systemic sexism if you believe in that but it's that they're all built around the male crashed crash-test dummy a 70 kilogram male is the physiological male and for some reason they focus more on that which means there are a lot of cases where it doesn't quite fit the individual really if you have a really safe car you'd have your seat belt and your seat position adjusted by an expert and tested for each individual shape and we can't do that manufacturing cars but you can do that new clinic with treatments you should be adjusting your treatment strategy for the individual in front of you not just giving a blanket same treatment for everyone okay so what kind of things would adjust or kind of factors and variables would make you adjust it if you think about four heads that probably the most variable area and they're the most difficult area because unlike you know treating with the bell oh you're trying you just trying to freeze that muscle from working they're basically four three muscles you basically relax all of them and you get

and that's all it takes to stop from someone from frowning with the forehead it's really different because there's actually it's actually one of the muscles where we actually want to just treat partially some of the muscle and leave the other half of the muscle functioning in some way so you need a little bit of muscle activity to support the eyebrows and give you some movement if you get rid of all of it you can get a brow ptosis so what we have then is a line between treated and untreated muscle and where that line is placed where that line runs is really crucial and a very small difference makes a big difference in the results so i brow asymmetry if you think about an injection on either side but there's a half a centimeter difference which looks like nothing you met you as the untrained eye would not spot it on treatment design but what you get is the patient coming back three weeks later saying look when I raise my eyebrow one goes up the centimeter more than the other one which is crucial there that's a really obvious difference you need to be able to make these little adjustments in order to come day for individuals faces if you look at the licenses there are two licenses for the forehead there's one for Boca chew and one for Botox and they are the Botox one in

particular is quite heavy in terms of dosages so you put in four units in five different places a four unit dose is relatively strong for the forehead I usually use about one unit for individual injection points but I spread them out a bit more so I might use it close to the same amount of product but I'm spreading it out it's more what's the way there's a higher resolution to my treatment than the four unit dose because a four unit dose if you're half a centimeter over to one side you're actually knocking out section more frontalis muscle and leaving a bit on the other side of it because you're so much in one spot mmm so this issue makes it harder for the for the new clinician whose resolution in terms of understanding the face isn't quite as detailed is that a few millimeters make a big difference so this is where it can become easier to separate your injections and to put more injections in more places because you're spreading things a little bit more over and your inaccuracies are blended you can able to blend it in a bit better we should say that we have an amazing download for you guys to

accompany this show which is Tim has it's literally it's funnel all the house pictures of celebrities and everything with their foreheads and it's the 26 essential injection patterns for Botox so if you go to the description below you can download that there so tell me then if I so I've got the patient in front of me and I'm in the process of making the decision about where I put them what can you recommend that I can physically do to this person to help me make that decision and lay out the patent well you need to first of all it's all about starts with the history what does the patient actually want everything we always say the real transformations are psychological transformation for the patient so that means what is their story what do they want you to achieve now with that will come something like oh I feel tired all the time now even though I'm not tired I look tired then you're obviously in it more likely to need an eyebrow lift so an eyebrow lift is immediately changing your injection strategy from removing lines and wrinkles and now obviously too

much of an eyebrow lift might cause lines that make them unhappy and so you mean immediately in this whole world of nuance right as opposed to here's my standard injection pattern so then you need to start to apply certain principles to that patient knowing what you know about the product characteristics and knowing what you know about their Anatomy and the interaction between the two because really what we're talking about is an interaction between Botox and the ANA and the anatomical picture you need to vary what you understand so that it as close as possible matches our patient so I'll give you an example of one of the foundation principles would be how far does the product spread for each injection so if you most people would say around one and a half to two centimeters in the fore you get spread around the point so that immediately tells you where you need to pay place products in order to relax the muscle the next question would be how much of that muscle do you actually want to relax because we need to leave some muscle functioning so that you don't get a brow ptosis you need to leave even more muscle functioning if you want an eyebrow lift and these things then need to become part of your treatment

plan because you're then deciding I always say one of the key things is for the forehead is deciding where you're not going to inject first because once you know where you're not gonna inject it's actually quite easy to put to dilute to do your treatment plan you then fill the area that you know you want to relax with products that fills about one to two centimeters per position and you can kind of think of it the area that you do want to relax almost like a little it's like a little glass that you want to fill with marbles each marbles one and a half to two centimeters and you're basically filling that and leaving the rest untreated and that's quite a good way of breaking the stages down what makes it a lot easier is if you decide to plan a journey so I'm gonna relax this section first make sure they don't get a brow drop and then add a bit more for example is a better way of doing it then saying I really don't want to spot brow so I'm going to put a little bit of Botox above your eyebrow above everyone's eyebrow because what you get instead of you will avoid all the spot brows but you'll also get a percentage of people who get a brow ptosis that wouldn't have had that and you're a bit more stuck with a brow Atlas than you are with a Spock

route because the Spock brow you can soften two weeks later so think of it as a journey and we're trying to find the safest journey from A to B we're not trying to get there at one go especially with a new client once you know their face then you can repeat you can do the same each time but you just don't want to take an under try and do too quickly and end up with a side effect that you could easily have avoided if you're taking a saber journey do you how would you mark up what do you do for both of us yeah so the first thing have a look at their face get ask them to move in all the muscles around the area that might be treated so not just a month the area they want to treat us and to do frown raise eyebrows up smile why so that you can see that interaction between the muscles I'll give you one example which is the orbicularis oculi muscle if that's really strong and you over treat the forehead is quite likely to contribute to ptosis so it'll actually pull the eyebrows down I learned this early on with someone who was actually afraid of looking over treated so we went really low dose in the forehead only four units which is nothing like the license doses 20 and she got a brow

ptosis and I couldn't believe it when I went to go and examine her afterwards this is my learning experience and she smiled I realized why she had really strong orbicularis oculi muscle so the balance between the lifting force orbicularis sorry the frontalis muscle and the depressing force which is mainly orbicularis oculi was basically slanted in the way of down so she got a brow ptosis with a low dose because of downward muscles were very strong so as soon as you know the balance between the muscles you can started very your treatment to make sure that you're maintaining harmony so how would you go about marking up so marking up is a process of combining the history and examination you want to be and remembering what they've told you about what they actually want and then applying that to the anatomy in front of you so I would like an eyebrow lift you then start out by marking out the risky area so where do you not want to inject for all the areas we want to leave for example one to one and a half to two centimeters from the orbital rim and then you want to mark out the areas of my calls of brow ptosis so there's a central component of your forehead that might call the drop if you inject in it

and there's the more lateral component if you want an eyebrow lift you need to leave the lateral muscle untreated so we're first deciding where we don't want to inject then we're relating that to the history the actual result the patient wants and you're trying to design a treatment that will relax exactly the right amount of muscle to get the treatment that you want with minimal risk and leaving room for a potential follow-up what about different sized foreheads I have quite a big forehead what about people who have big and small yeah well we were talking about this for some reason people tend to notice for it I know you're saying I can never remember which is which and Zac sundeck yeah that's got big forward yeah you could remember no it's aunt it's I'm sorry exactly no they're both forwards get everyone's all men's forward get bigger as they lose hair as well as they received but certainly in which one is a deck is no aunt and has famously got a big forehead and you could compare him to who's the others over tea with this more forehead were talking small forehead yeah Zayn

Malik off of one direction yeah so if you just look at their two fours I'm sure David will get these two up together you can look at those two faces and see that you can't treat them the same one has got a lot more floor space so to speak a bigger deck may your aunt begin and you're gonna have to use it you if you think the product spreads one and a half centimeters or so you're gonna need more to cover the same area right so this immediately changes things and this is why Ford's are quite a good example of why you can't use a standard formula because there's such a huge variation well I'm sure David will show a few more pictures of some piece of varieties of forehead that this is a bond or clear indication that you need to be nuanced in your treatment and but yeah that's the key thing you need more in bigger foreheads and bigger muscles the other interesting thing about men is they tend have a different shape for tireless and they often have a muscle that goes much higher up so if you often get this in the beginning when people first start practicing as they mainly see women because 90% of

mark is women and they will leave the upper triangle and then men get these little horns and that come out and you end up them having to follow them more of them up and they're a little bit grumpy when they come back because I've had these weird pulls at the top yeah okay so jumping off that then follow ups tell us your thoughts about how let's take it from this angle what how can you learn from your follow ups really few in the habit as I train all our training series of taking a picture of your treatment plan before you do the treatment so mark out then take a picture then do the treatment when they come back you've got a beautiful way of finding out exactly what one unit or four units does in their face and that will give you the ability to make adjustments and to learn really quickly exactly the interaction between what you're doing and the result because the hardest thing about Botox is that you don't get the results straight away so dermal filler you inject you get the result you want or you don't get the result and then you try again with Botox as a two week lag time so you've got to do something then see what happens and then learn from it and it takes the

learning process is a hundred times harder if you don't know exactly what you did so take a picture then compare the result and then you can also then combine the two together so you end up with two treatment plans that you've combined into one because you'll follow up your original treatment is basically one treatment plan that can reduce your follow-up rates saves you time in your clinic you should also get your treatment your follow-up rate right down to like 10 to 15 percent yes interesting I've seen a few different clinicians describe this in different ways and one of our clinicians I remember when she came on board with us it's can be ver she was a bit like dudes you're doing a lot of follow-ups do you really need to be doing this are you being too cautious in the first go but then I have heard other clinicians and I'm sure a drug rep said to me once oh we know follow-ups are good because you get an opportunity to kind of bond with the client more and you can maybe you know talk about other treatments and stuff what do you think about follow ups well it depends how you look at it from a purely business perspective follow-ups are football and people coming back to

your clinic to see you again you can you're probably you're kind of indoctrinating people into visiting you frequently on the other side of that no one wants to waste their time and if you have if you're coming back repeatedly and not get anything for it most clients will just stop coming back for the follow-up and s they get value from it there are other clients who don't need to follow up you get habitual eyes dead and will come up no matter what no matter how much you tweak the first treatment they always come back and there's also compulsion on clinics to validate their effort that they've gone through and just do a little tweak and you can train people to come back and there's ultimately very little value in it so talk to me about the crow's feet so crow's feet otherwise called lateral canthal lines are the lines that come off laterally to your eye and they are associated with obviously with smiling but obviously they're aging as well so and we need to sometimes treat them in certain patients and it should improve fine lines around the eyes the interesting thing about crow's feet is that you can get rid of lines

but also I think is one of the area's that's heart this most easy to make someone look basically a botoxed hmm because but in a subtle way so it's not the kind of area that you look frozen but you change the dynamic of the face in a way that people who know you well can detect and certainly we've experienced yeah the first time you had a treatment and I was joke that the first thing I felt is that you weren't laughing at my jokes and so then that created an element of doubt every time you crack a really funny don't get the response DUI so that but that's kind of subtle the patient almost never notice is that so I've been come increasingly careful when I'm consulting people to bring in this dynamic of you know that think about your face is a functioning unit not necessarily just as a place where wrinkles come and need to be stamped out because I'd the ideal result as a balance where you have expression but you don't get lines and the crucial thing for me I think is you need to be able to see narrowing of the white part

of your eye and while you smile if you get a complete stunning so it's almost like if you had a something covering this part of the face and they smile and you can't see it then you've ever treated because it's kind of they saw a soulless smile earlier you said that the frown line was the most sort of simple to treat so what would you say about that so fine lines are probably the simplest to treat because you're just making a the entire muscle group still so unlike orbicularis oculi and the frontalis muscle where you're actually only relaxing part of the muscle the corrugator complex can be completely relaxed you're treating press eres the corrugator super silly and the depressor super silly and all of those can be made still without causing anything noticeable so even an expert unless they say frown for me they won't be able to tell whereas and because of the other expressions like eyebrow movements the best example we tend to use our eyebrows almost constantly when you're talking that flicker if there's something going on in my mind this processing going on that's what we're detecting with eyebrow movement and so

if you completely still it particularly if someone is particularly for people who know them well they might think they're not thinking in the same way and it I don't think it always creates and basically it like people don't always notice it consciously but I think it might create a sense of uncertainty with people that they're not reacting in the same way I certainly remember right the beginning my career meeting someone who had the sense that I couldn't trust them yes um and with that person it turned out that I couldn't trust them I think I know how you talk about but I also remember thinking is this just the boat that's confusing me because I can't read them what about Bonnie lines they're little annoying thing yeah so that they seem to come on when you treat other areas of the face they get worse so if you treat the orbicularis oculi often the medial part of the face gets more active and that's something to do with the ability of toxin or the basically or no nervous system responds by putting more effort into the places that are untreated so it can happen with

frown lines and with crow's feet that when people smile after the treatment they actually have these new lines which developed as a Botox look and you often see people who think they look Botox that's one of the key giveaways so you can reduce the effect of it maybe by using less you're maintaining balance so putting less around orbicularis oculi might cause less lines up on the nose or you can just treat the little lines on the nose as well I kind of think of them almost as if the orbicularis oculi is going all the way around the nose I know some of it's probably in a Zyliss but the level of the muscle is relatively superficial and it seems to behave like one unit so I inject really superficially and you're the crucial thing with bundi lines is you want to be really careful not to affect the lip elevators as one of the worst side effects of Bunnie lines if you treat a little bit inferior laterally then you might affect the muscles that elevate the lip and you get often it's asymmetrical so when they smile afterwards one side goes up on the other side still it's an awful side effect so thank you so much for watching I hope you've enjoyed this episode you

can get more information from the detailed download remember it's in the links below and we love your comments let us know what you think and we'll add them into the next show so anything interesting we might do a whole show about it you never know but do let us know what you're thinking if we can help you in any way don't forget as well at this esthetics mastery show goes out as a podcast so we are available on any of the normal podcast platforms Spotify iTunes SoundCloud wherever you get your podcasts we are there so if you want to listen to us on your commute or in the bath or whatever you are welcome and please do drop us like add a subscription it really helps us to know that you guys are enjoying this and if not let us know as well we can always improve thanks for watching take care [Music]
you
